title: frangula paruensis
intro: Frangula paruensis, also known as the Mountain Buckthorn, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Frangula paruensis include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Frangula paruensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
